title: Idempotency in payments
description: An idempotency key lets a payment be retried safely so network failures do not double-charge.
body: Use when a payment request may be retried after an ambiguous failure. An idempotency key identifies one logical attempt: the processor (and your own records) return the original outcome on a repeat key instead of charging again. Do not retry a charge without one — a timeout that actually succeeded then charges twice. Actions: persist the key before calling the processor and dedupe on it. Validation signals include a repeated request yielding one charge. Risk boundary: payment execution is advisory and defers to business.finance with a human gate.
